To a solution of benzoic acid (122 mg, 1.00 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and aniline (102 mg, 1.10 mmol, 1.1 equiv) in DMF (5 mL) was added HATU (456 mg, 1.20 mmol, 1.2 equiv) and N,N-diisopropylethylamine (517 mg, 4.00 mmol, 4.0 equiv) at room temperature. The mixture was stirred for 4 h, then diluted with EtOAc (30 mL), washed with sat. NaHCO3 (2 × 20 mL) and brine (20 mL), dried over Na2SO4, filtered, and concentrated under reduced pressure. The crude residue was purified by flash chromatography (silica gel, hexanes/EtOAc, 70:30) to afford N-phenylbenzamide as a white solid (181 mg, 92%).
